title: jatropha neopauciflora
intro: Jatropha neopauciflora, also known as the dwarf Barbados nut or dwarf stinking rose, is a plant species native to Mexico and Central America. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant's health benefits include its ability to treat various ailments such as wounds, skin conditions, and gastrointestinal issues. Herbal preparations of Jatropha neopauciflora are typically made by drying the leaves and roots, then infusing them in water or oil to create teas, tinctures, and salves for topical use.
